text.title: Annual Planning Process — Agent Rules
text.summary: Safety rules and prompt patterns for agents driving year-in-review, OKR drafting, and review-cycle automation.

rule.statement: Agents produce year-in-review drafts, OKR candidates, and review packets — all as drafts for human review. Agents do not set vision, make strategic pivots, close objectives, or make capital-allocation decisions.
rule.rationale: Vision is a founder artifact. Autonomous closure of objectives hides drift in inputs. Strategic pivots on narrative/qualitative signals require founder ownership.

rule.statement: Force quantification: if an LLM-proposed KR contains vague language ("increase meaningfully", "improve significantly") without a number + unit + date, reject it and request a rewrite. Do not accept hedged targets.
rule.rationale: Vague KRs are the number-one planning anti-pattern. Auto-hedging is LLM-default behavior that must be actively suppressed.

rule.statement: Red-team every annual plan by enumerating 5 failure modes that would cause the plan to fail by Q3. For each, name the leading indicator and the kill-switch. Surface as a risk register, not as plan edits.
rule.rationale: Forward planning without adversarial review produces plans that assume best-case execution. The red-team pass surfaces brittle assumptions before they cost a quarter.

section.title: Prompt patterns

example.note: Year-in-review prompt: "Goal: produce year-in-review draft for [Year]. Inputs: monthly metric exports (revenue, customers, churn, MRR, ARPU); CRM win/loss notes; sprint retros. Method: compute YoY deltas; surface 3 wins, 3 challenges, 3 surprises with CITED data; flag any metric definition that changed mid-year. Constraints: no more than 1 page; every claim sourced; mark ASSUMPTION when data is missing."

Red-team prompt: "Goal: red-team annual plan v1. Method: enumerate 5 ways this plan fails by Q3. For each, name the leading indicator, the kill-switch, and the alternative. Refuse: if priorities are more than 3, refuse and ask user to drop one; do not silently accept. Output: risk register; do not edit the plan."

section.reference: Monthly check-in: actual vs expected KR progress with on-track/at-risk/off-track status
